   325	                         sym_tol=1e-9, tag="reported_cov"):
   326	    require_square_finite(C, expected_dim, tag)
   327	    if symmetry_rel(C) > sym_tol:
   328	        raise FpsGateError(f"{tag}: rel asymmetry {symmetry_rel(C):.2e} > {sym_tol:.1e}")
   329	    if mask_hash_actual != mask_hash_expected:
   330	        raise FpsGateError(
   331	            f"{tag}: reported-mask hash {mask_hash_actual} != manifest {mask_hash_expected}")
   332	    return True
   333	
   334	
   335	def check_active_rollup(per_band, total, tol=1e-10):
   336	    """The active five-band lateral rollup must have EXACTLY the 5 named bands, each with a
   337	    finite nonzero-trace PSD-ish block, and total == sum(5 bands) within tol. A zero band or
   338	    zero total (the 'nothing happened' failure) raises."""
   339	    got = set(per_band)
   340	    want = set(BANDS)
   341	    if got != want:
   342	        raise FpsGateError(
   343	            f"active rollup bands != 5 named; missing={sorted(want - got)} extra={sorted(got - want)}")
   344	    acc = None
   345	    for b in BANDS:
   346	        cb = np.asarray(per_band[b], float)
   347	        require_square_finite(cb, tag=f"active band {b}")
   348	        tr = float(np.trace(cb))
   349	        if not np.isfinite(tr) or tr <= 0:
   350	            raise FpsGateError(f"active band {b}: trace {tr:.3e} <= 0 (band contributes nothing)")
   351	        acc = cb.copy() if acc is None else acc + cb
   352	    total = np.asarray(total, float)
   353	    require_square_finite(total, expected_dim=acc.shape[0], tag="active total")
   354	    if float(np.trace(total)) <= 0:
   355	        raise FpsGateError("active total: trace <= 0")
   356	    resid = float(np.max(np.abs(total - acc)))
   357	    denom = max(1e-300, float(np.max(np.abs(total))))
   358	    if resid / denom > tol:
   359	        raise FpsGateError(
   360	            f"active total != sum(5 bands): max|diff|={resid:.3e} rel={resid/denom:.2e} > {tol:.1e}")
   361	    return True
   362	
   363	
   364	def pure_sum_vs_sub_residual(C_sum, C_sub):
   365	    C_sum = np.asarray(C_sum, float); C_sub = np.asarray(C_sub, float)
   366	    if C_sum.shape != C_sub.shape:
   367	        raise FpsGateError(f"sum/sub shape mismatch {C_sum.shape} vs {C_sub.shape}")
   368	    resid = float(np.max(np.abs(C_sum - C_sub)))
   369	    denom = max(1e-300, float(np.max(np.abs(C_sum))))
   370	    return resid, resid / denom
   371	
   372	
   373	def require_pure_sum_matches_sub(C_sum, C_sub, tol=1e-9):
   374	    """The PSD-safe pure-sum swap must equal the same-source subtraction to float precision.
   375	    A large residual means the stored combined is NOT SUM(bands)+stat+ML (evidence problem),
   376	    so the pure sum cannot be trusted -> fail closed."""
   377	    resid, rel = pure_sum_vs_sub_residual(C_sum, C_sub)
   378	    if rel > tol:
   379	        raise FpsGateError(
   380	            f"pure-sum vs same-source-subtraction residual rel={rel:.3e} > {tol:.1e} "
   381	            f"(abs {resid:.3e}); stored combined is not a clean component sum")
   382	    return resid, rel
   383	
   384	
   385	def require_unified_inputs(diag_block, diag_unified, tag="unified"):
   386	    """Reject the pathological throw comparator where a block-sum variance is 0 while the unified
   387	    variance is >0 (division/inflation would be ill-defined; signals a mask/estimator mismatch)."""
   388	    db = np.asarray(diag_block, float); du = np.asarray(diag_unified, float)
   389	    if db.shape != du.shape:
   390	        raise FpsGateError(f"{tag}: block/unified diag shape mismatch {db.shape} vs {du.shape}")
   391	    bad = np.where((db <= 0) & (du > 0))[0]
   392	    if bad.size:
   393	        raise FpsGateError(
   394	            f"{tag}: {bad.size} bins have diag(C_blocksum)<=0 < diag(C_unified) "
   395	            f"(first bins {bad[:5].tolist()})")
   396	    return True
   397	
   398	
   399	def require_mean_shift(arr, expected_dim=None, tag="hJointMeanShift"):
   400	    """hJointMeanShift is MANDATORY for the unified adoption: present, non-empty, finite, and (if
   401	    given) of the expected dimension. Preserved separately under the mean-centered policy."""
   402	    if arr is None:
   403	        raise FpsGateError(f"{tag} absent (mandatory for unified adoption)")
   404	    a = np.asarray(arr, float)
   405	    if a.size == 0:
   406	        raise FpsGateError(f"{tag} empty")
   407	    if not np.all(np.isfinite(a)):
   408	        raise FpsGateError(f"{tag} has non-finite entries")
   409	    if expected_dim is not None and a.size != expected_dim:
   410	        raise FpsGateError(f"{tag} dim {a.size} != {expected_dim}")
   411	    return True
   412	
   413	
   414	def require_no_path_alias(out_path, *in_paths):
   415	    o = os.path.realpath(out_path)
   416	    for p in in_paths:
   417	        if p and os.path.realpath(p) == o:
   418	            raise FpsGateError(f"path alias: output {out_path} aliases input {p}")
   419	    return True
   420	
   421	
   422	def reconstruct_final(C_pre, C_vertical, g):
   423	    """C_final = C_pre - C_vertical + (g g^T) o C_vertical  (unified-throw inflation adoption)."""
   424	    C_pre = np.asarray(C_pre, float); C_vertical = np.asarray(C_vertical, float)
   425	    g = np.asarray(g, float)
   426	    G = np.outer(g, g)
   427	    return C_pre - C_vertical + G * C_vertical
   428	
   429	
   430	def require_final_identity(C_final, C_pre, C_vertical, g, tol=1e-9, tag="final adoption"):
   431	    C_ref = _sym(reconstruct_final(C_pre, C_vertical, g))
   432	    C_fin = _sym(np.asarray(C_final, float))
   433	    resid = float(np.max(np.abs(C_fin - C_ref)))
   434	    denom = max(1e-300, float(np.max(np.abs(C_ref))))
   435	    if resid / denom > tol:
   436	        raise FpsGateError(
   437	            f"{tag}: reconstructed C_pre - C_vert + (gg^T)oC_vert != final "
   438	            f"(rel {resid/denom:.3e} > {tol:.1e})")
   439	    return resid
   440	
   441	
   442	def cov_fingerprint(C, mask_hash_val=None):
   443	    """Compact, reproducible fingerprint block for receipts."""
   444	    C = _sym(np.asarray(C, float))
   445	    ev = np.linalg.eigvalsh(C)
   446	    d = np.diag(C)
   447	    return {
   448	        "dim": int(C.shape[0]),
   449	        "rank": int(np.sum(ev > ev.max() * 1e-12)) if ev.size and ev.max() > 0 else 0,
   450	        "sqrt_trace": float(np.sqrt(max(np.trace(C), 0.0))),
   451	        "min_over_max_eig": float(ev[0] / max(1e-300, abs(ev[-1]))),
   452	        "symmetry_rel": symmetry_rel(C),
   453	        "n_pos_diag": int(np.sum(d > 0)),
   454	        "mask_hash": mask_hash_val,
   455	    }

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Blocker 4 (Agent C, 2nd repair round): READ-ONLY reuse + verification of the orchestrator's
     3	owner-neutral full-file merged-input hash receipt (docs/orchestration/state/merged-input-hashes/
     4	p4-merged-20260718/). Avoids a second 748GB hash pass: it recomputes only live size + integer-mtime
     5	against the committed inventory and binds the committed hash-list / inventory digests.
     6	
     7	Requires COMPLETE, summary.tsv, validation.tsv (result=PASS), fps.sha256, fps.inventory.tsv. For each
     8	of the ten FPS merged inputs it checks the file exists and its live (size, int(mtime)) equals the
     9	inventory row (i.e. unchanged since the validated hashing), then verifies sha256(fps.sha256) ==
    10	validation.fps_hash_list_sha256 and sha256(fps.inventory.tsv) == validation.fps_inventory_sha256.
    11	Exposes load_verified_input_hashes() -> {abs_path: full_sha256} for the publication manifest builder.
    12	
    13	Fail-closed: any missing file, non-PASS validation, size/mtime drift, or digest mismatch raises.
    14	
    15	  python fps_verify_merged_receipt.py         # prints bound digests + 10/10 size/mtime OK
    16	"""
    17	import os
    18	import sys
    19	
    20	import fps_provenance as fp
    21	
    22	REPO = "/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold"
    23	RECEIPT = os.path.join(REPO, "docs/orchestration/state/merged-input-hashes/p4-merged-20260718")
    24	REQUIRED = ["COMPLETE", "summary.tsv", "validation.tsv", "fps.sha256", "fps.inventory.tsv"]
    25	
    26	
    27	def _read_kv_tsv(path):
    28	    d = {}
    29	    for line in open(path):
    30	        parts = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
    31	        if len(parts) >= 2:
    32	            d[parts[0]] = parts[1]
    33	    return d
    34	
    35	
    36	def verify(receipt=RECEIPT):
    37	    for f in REQUIRED:
    38	        if not os.path.exists(os.path.join(receipt, f)):
    39	            raise fp.FpsGateError(f"receipt: required file absent: {f}")
    40	    val = _read_kv_tsv(os.path.join(receipt, "validation.tsv"))
    41	    if val.get("result") != "PASS":
    42	        raise fp.FpsGateError(f"receipt validation result != PASS ({val.get('result')})")
    43	
    44	    # bind committed digests (recompute, don't trust strings)
    45	    sha_list = os.path.join(receipt, "fps.sha256")
    46	    inv = os.path.join(receipt, "fps.inventory.tsv")
    47	    hl = fp.sha256_file(sha_list)
    48	    iv = fp.sha256_file(inv)
    49	    if hl != val.get("fps_hash_list_sha256"):
    50	        raise fp.FpsGateError(
    51	            f"fps.sha256 digest {hl} != validation.fps_hash_list_sha256 {val.get('fps_hash_list_sha256')}")
    52	    if iv != val.get("fps_inventory_sha256"):
    53	        raise fp.FpsGateError(
    54	            f"fps.inventory.tsv digest {iv} != validation.fps_inventory_sha256 {val.get('fps_inventory_sha256')}")
    55	
    56	    # recompute live size + integer mtime vs inventory (cheap; proves files unchanged since hashing)
    57	    inv_rows = {}
    58	    for line in open(inv):
    59	        c = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
    60	        if len(c) >= 3:
    61	            inv_rows[c[2]] = (int(c[0]), int(c[1]))     # rel_path -> (size, int_mtime)
    62	    hashes = {}
    63	    for line in open(sha_list):
    64	        c = line.rstrip("\n").split()
    65	        if len(c) >= 2:
    66	            hashes[c[1]] = c[0]                          # rel_path -> sha256
    67	    fps_rows = {p: v for p, v in inv_rows.items() if "/fps/merged/" in p}
    68	    if len(fps_rows) != fp.N_ENDPOINTS:
    69	        raise fp.FpsGateError(f"receipt fps inventory has {len(fps_rows)} rows != {fp.N_ENDPOINTS}")
    70	    verified = {}
    71	    for rel, (size, mt) in sorted(fps_rows.items()):
    72	        ap = os.path.join(REPO, rel)
    73	        if not os.path.exists(ap):
    74	            raise fp.FpsGateError(f"receipt: merged input missing on disk: {rel}")
    75	        st = os.stat(ap)
    76	        if st.st_size != size:
    77	            raise fp.FpsGateError(f"{rel}: live size {st.st_size} != inventory {size}")
    78	        if int(st.st_mtime) != mt:
    79	            raise fp.FpsGateError(f"{rel}: live int(mtime) {int(st.st_mtime)} != inventory {mt}")
    80	        if rel not in hashes:
    81	            raise fp.FpsGateError(f"{rel}: present in inventory but absent from fps.sha256")
    82	        verified[ap] = hashes[rel]
    83	    return {
    84	        "receipt_dir": receipt,
    85	        "run_id": open(os.path.join(receipt, "COMPLETE")).read().strip(),
    86	        "fps_hash_list_sha256": hl,

    98	def main():
    99	    r = verify()
   100	    print(f"[receipt] PASS  run_id={r['run_id']}  fps_bytes={r['fps_bytes']}")
   101	    print(f"[receipt] fps_hash_list_sha256={r['fps_hash_list_sha256']}")
   102	    print(f"[receipt] fps_inventory_sha256={r['fps_inventory_sha256']}")
   103	    print(f"[receipt] launcher_sha256={r['launcher_sha256']}")
   104	    print(f"[receipt] 10/10 live size+int(mtime) match inventory; full input SHA256 bound:")

     2	"""Blocker 1/4 (Agent C, 2nd repair round): compute + COMMIT the exact 266/285 FPS reported mask
     3	(CV>0 on the 285-bin C-order extended grid) BEFORE any endpoint production, and lock the fingerprint
     4	construction to the orchestrator/verifier canonical value

     6	Tries a set of candidate constructions over the CV>0 mask and reports which reproduces TARGET, then
     7	writes a read-only artifact active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/fps_reported_mask.json bound to the CV
     8	sha256. Needs ROOT (26KB CV read) -> runs on a compute node.
     9	
    10	  python fps_reported_mask.py --cv <CV.root> --out active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/fps_reported_mask.json
    11	"""
    12	import argparse
    13	import hashlib
    14	import json
    15	import os
    16	
    17	import numpy as np
    18	import ROOT
    19	
    20	import fps_provenance as fp
    21	
    22	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    23	TARGET = "23b2a2f4e75f242141c0651052258a930ed38abc4f1740afaa0b349da78bda78"
    24	
    25	
    26	def _h(b):
    27	    return hashlib.sha256(b).hexdigest()
    28	
    29	
    30	def candidates(mask):
    31	    idx = np.where(mask)[0]
    32	    return {
    33	        "bool_tobytes": _h(mask.astype(bool).tobytes()),
    34	        "uint8_tobytes": _h(mask.astype(np.uint8).tobytes()),
    35	        "packbits": _h(np.packbits(mask.astype(bool)).tobytes()),
    36	        "idx_int64": _h(idx.astype("<i8").tobytes()),
    37	        "idx_int32": _h(idx.astype("<i4").tobytes()),
    38	        "idx_csv": _h(",".join(map(str, idx.tolist())).encode()),
    39	        "idx_newline": _h("\n".join(map(str, idx.tolist())).encode()),
    40	        "idx_newline_trail": _h(("\n".join(map(str, idx.tolist())) + "\n").encode()),
    41	        "bitstring01": _h("".join("1" if x else "0" for x in mask).encode()),
    42	        "bitstring01_nl": _h(("".join("1" if x else "0" for x in mask) + "\n").encode()),
    43	        "n_slash_N_idxcsv": _h((f"{int(mask.sum())}/{mask.size}:" +
    44	                                ",".join(map(str, idx.tolist()))).encode()),
    45	        "space_join_idx": _h(" ".join(map(str, idx.tolist())).encode()),
    46	    }
    47	
    48	
    49	def main():
    50	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    51	    ap.add_argument("--cv", default="uq_fps/universe_sweep/fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_CV.root")
    52	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/fps_reported_mask.json")
    53	    a = ap.parse_args()
    54	
    55	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(a.cv)
    56	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    57	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] cannot open CV {a.cv}")
    58	    h = f.Get("hXSecND_flat")
    59	    if not h:
    60	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] no hXSecND_flat in {a.cv}")
    61	    nb = h.GetNbinsX()
    62	    cv = np.array([h.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(nb)])
    63	    f.Close()
    64	    if nb != fp.NBINS_EXT:
    65	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] CV nbins {nb} != {fp.NBINS_EXT}")
    66	    mask = cv > 0
    67	    n = int(mask.sum())
    68	    cv_sha = fp.sha256_file(a.cv)
    69	    print(f"[mask] CV={a.cv} nbins={nb} reported(CV>0)={n}  cv_sha256={cv_sha[:16]}")
    70	
    71	    cand = candidates(mask)
    72	    winner = None
    73	    for name, val in cand.items():
    74	        flag = "  <== MATCHES TARGET" if val == TARGET else ""
    75	        print(f"   {name:22s} {val}{flag}")
    76	        if val == TARGET:
    77	            winner = name
    78	    if winner is None:
    79	        print(f"\n[mask] NO candidate construction reproduced TARGET {TARGET}")
    80	        print("[mask] EVIDENCE-BLOCKED: cannot lock the reported-mask fingerprint construction")
    81	        raise SystemExit(4)
    82	
    83	    art = {
    84	        "schema": "fps_reported_mask.v1", "readonly": True,
    85	        "n_reported": n, "n_total": nb, "ravel_order": fp.RAVEL_ORDER,
    86	        "reported_indices": np.where(mask)[0].tolist(),
    87	        "fingerprint": TARGET, "fingerprint_construction": winner,
    88	        "central_cv": a.cv, "central_cv_sha256": cv_sha,
    89	        "layout_fingerprint": fp.layout_fingerprint(),
    90	    }
    91	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(a.out), exist_ok=True)
    92	    if os.path.exists(a.out):
    93	        os.chmod(a.out, 0o644)
    94	    with open(a.out, "w") as fh:
    95	        json.dump(art, fh, indent=2)
    96	    os.chmod(a.out, 0o444)
    97	    print(f"\n[mask] LOCKED construction='{winner}' == TARGET")
    98	    print(f"[mask] wrote read-only {a.out}  (n_reported={n}/{nb})")
    99	
   100	
   101	if __name__ == "__main__":
   102	    main()

     2	"""Fail-closed provenance + gate library for the FPS selection-complete lateral covariance
     3	chain (Agent C, P4-FPS / P6-FPS-final). ROOT-FREE by design: pure python + hashlib + numpy so
     4	every gate is unit-testable without a compute node, and the ROOT-facing scripts (rollup /
     5	validator / adopt / unified-adopt) call these same functions after loading matrices via ROOT.
     6	
     7	Motivation (2026-07-18 reconciliation): the ten existing FPS endpoint unfolds were produced with
     8	the unfold default --bkg-mode=purity (both launchers omit --bkg-mode), so they are PURITY CONTROLS,
     9	not publication inputs. The selected publication footing is 'negweight-refined'. These gates make
    10	that distinction structural: a purity/default/missing background mode CANNOT enter a publication
    11	rollup, and every covariance step fails closed on inventory / order / mask / footing / identity.
    12	
    13	Every `require_*` raises FpsGateError (fail-closed) with an explicit message; never returns a soft

    15	"""

    17	import json

    20	import numpy as np

    22	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- canonical layout
    23	# FPS extended grid (from both FPS unfold launchers; C-order ravel of (pt, pz)).

    25	            1.00, 1.25, 1.50, 2.50, 4.50, 30.0]                       # 16 edges -> 15 pt bins
    26	PZ_EDGES = [0.0, 0.75, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 4.5, 5.0,
    27	            6.0, 7.0, 8.0, 9.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0, 40.0, 60.0, 120.0]  # 20 edges -> 19 pz bins
    28	NPT = len(PT_EDGES) - 1          # 15
    29	NPZ = len(PZ_EDGES) - 1          # 19
    30	NBINS_EXT = NPT * NPZ            # 285 (extended grid, C-order)
    31	RAVEL_ORDER = "C"
    32	
    33	# the 5 selection-complete lateral bands x the 2 endpoints

    35	         "Muon_Energy_MINERvA", "Muon_Energy_MINOS"]
    36	ENDPOINTS = (0, 1)
    37	N_ENDPOINTS = len(BANDS) * len(ENDPOINTS)   # 10
    38	
    39	# execution footing the publication rollup demands
    40	PUBLICATION_BKG_MODE = "negweight-refined"
    41	CONTROL_BKG_MODE = "purity"
    42	REQUIRED_FOOTING = {
    43	    "estimator": "lgbm",
    44	    "seed": 42,
    45	    "iters": 5,
    46	    "use_weights": True,
    47	    "full_phase_space": True,

    51	# manifest schemas / labels
    52	CONTROL_SCHEMA = "fps_endpoint_manifest.v1"
    53	PUBLICATION_SCHEMA = "fps_endpoint_manifest.v2"
    54	CONTROL_LABEL = "purity-control"
    55	PUBLICATION_LABEL = "publication"
    56	
    57	# canonical 266/285 reported-mask fingerprint (orchestrator/verifier-supplied; recompute + compare,
    58	# never trust a string). mask_fingerprint() below reproduces this from the CV>0 mask; the exact
    59	# construction is locked by fps_reported_mask.py against this target.
    60	REPORTED_MASK_FINGERPRINT = "23b2a2f4e75f242141c0651052258a930ed38abc4f1740afaa0b349da78bda78"
    61	N_REPORTED = 266
    62	
    63	# every publication endpoint ROOT must bind these full hashes (no deferred/partial/None allowed)
    64	PUBLICATION_ENDPOINT_HASH_FIELDS = [
    65	    "unfold_sha256", "input_merged_sha256", "config_sha256",
    66	    "source_sha256", "launcher_sha256", "central_sha256", "audit_sha256",
    67	]

    70	
    71	class FpsGateError(Exception):
    72	    """Raised by any fail-closed gate. Message names the exact artifact + failure."""
    73	
    74	
    75	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- hashing helpers
    76	def sha256_file(path):
    77	    if not os.path.exists(path):
    78	        raise FpsGateError(f"sha256_file: missing artifact {path}")
    79	    h = hashlib.sha256()
    80	    with open(path, "rb") as f:
    81	        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(1 << 20), b""):
    82	            h.update(chunk)
    83	    return h.hexdigest()
    84	
    85	
    86	def sha256_bytes(b):
    87	    return hashlib.sha256(b).hexdigest()
    88	
    89	
    90	def sha256_partial(path, edge_bytes=8 << 20):
    91	    """Bounded content fingerprint (size + head + tail) for very large files where a full-file
    92	    SHA256 is impractical (the 74GB merged endpoint inputs = 740GB of login-node I/O for all ten).
    93	    NOT a full hash: labeled 'partial-headtail' so a caller can never mistake it for one; the full
    94	    input identity is corroborated by the merged-endpoint audit receipt. A full SHA256 remains a
    95	    recorded residual evidence gap until it can be computed on a compute node."""
    96	    if not os.path.exists(path):
    97	        raise FpsGateError(f"sha256_partial: missing artifact {path}")
    98	    sz = os.path.getsize(path)
    99	    h = hashlib.sha256()
   100	    h.update(f"size={sz};".encode())
   101	    with open(path, "rb") as f:
   102	        h.update(f.read(min(edge_bytes, sz)))
   103	        if sz > edge_bytes:
   104	            f.seek(max(0, sz - edge_bytes))
   105	            h.update(f.read(edge_bytes))
   106	    return f"partial-headtail{edge_bytes}b:{h.hexdigest()}"
   107	
   108	
   109	def layout_fingerprint(pt_edges=PT_EDGES, pz_edges=PZ_EDGES):
   110	    """Deterministic hash binding the exact edges + C-order + 285-bin layout."""
   111	    payload = json.dumps(
   112	        {"pt_edges": [float(x) for x in pt_edges],
   113	         "pz_edges": [float(x) for x in pz_edges],
   114	         "npt": len(pt_edges) - 1, "npz": len(pz_edges) - 1,
   115	         "nbins": (len(pt_edges) - 1) * (len(pz_edges) - 1),
   116	         "ravel_order": RAVEL_ORDER},
   117	        sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":")).encode()
   118	    return sha256_bytes(payload)
   119	
   120	
   121	def mask_hash(mask_bool):
   122	    """Hash of the reported-bin boolean mask (285 -> reported). Binds a covariance to the exact
   123	    CV>0 reporting mask so a covariance built against a different central can never be adopted."""
   124	    m = np.asarray(mask_bool, dtype=bool)
   125	    if m.ndim != 1:
   126	        raise FpsGateError(f"mask_hash: mask must be 1-D, got {m.shape}")
   127	    return sha256_bytes(m.tobytes()) + f":n{int(m.sum())}/{m.size}"
   128	
   129	
   130	def mask_fingerprint(mask_bool):
   131	    """CANONICAL reported-mask fingerprint: sha256 of the 285-length boolean mask as uint8 bytes
   132	    (C-order). Construction locked by fps_reported_mask.py against the verifier target
   133	    REPORTED_MASK_FINGERPRINT (23b2a2f4...). Builders RECOMPUTE this from CV>0 and compare -- they
   134	    never trust the manifest string alone."""
   135	    m = np.asarray(mask_bool, dtype=bool)
   136	    if m.ndim != 1:
   137	        raise FpsGateError(f"mask_fingerprint: mask must be 1-D, got {m.shape}")
   138	    return sha256_bytes(m.astype(np.uint8).tobytes())
   139	
   140	
   141	def require_reported_mask(mask_bool):
   142	    """Recompute the reported-mask fingerprint from a boolean mask and require it to equal the
   143	    canonical value; also require exactly N_REPORTED (266) true bins on the 285-bin grid."""
   144	    m = np.asarray(mask_bool, dtype=bool)
   145	    if m.size != NBINS_EXT:
   146	        raise FpsGateError(f"reported mask size {m.size} != {NBINS_EXT}")
   147	    if int(m.sum()) != N_REPORTED:
   148	        raise FpsGateError(f"reported mask has {int(m.sum())} bins != {N_REPORTED}")
   149	    fpv = mask_fingerprint(m)
   150	    if fpv != REPORTED_MASK_FINGERPRINT:
   151	        raise FpsGateError(
   152	            f"recomputed reported-mask fingerprint {fpv} != canonical {REPORTED_MASK_FINGERPRINT}")
   153	    return fpv
   154	
   155	
   156	def endpoint_key(band, ep):
   157	    return f"{band}_{ep}"
   158	
   159	
   160	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- manifest gates
   161	def require_manifest_inventory(manifest):
   162	    """Exactly the 10 named (band, endpoint) entries: no missing, extra, or duplicate."""
   163	    entries = manifest.get("endpoints")
   164	    if not isinstance(entries, list):
   165	        raise FpsGateError("manifest: 'endpoints' missing or not a list")
   166	    keys = [f"{e.get('band')}_{e.get('endpoint')}" for e in entries]
   167	    if len(keys) != len(set(keys)):
   168	        dup = sorted({k for k in keys if keys.count(k) > 1})
   169	        raise FpsGateError(f"manifest inventory: duplicate endpoints {dup}")
   170	    want = {endpoint_key(b, ep) for b in BANDS for ep in ENDPOINTS}
   171	    got = set(keys)
   172	    if got != want:
   173	        raise FpsGateError(
   174	            f"manifest inventory != exactly 10 named endpoints; "
   175	            f"missing={sorted(want - got)} extra={sorted(got - want)}")
   176	    if len(entries) != N_ENDPOINTS:
   177	        raise FpsGateError(f"manifest inventory: {len(entries)} entries != {N_ENDPOINTS}")
   178	    return True
   179	
   180	
   181	def require_common_fingerprints(manifest):
   182	    """All entries + the manifest header agree on layout, reported-mask, and central hashes."""
   183	    for key in ("layout_fingerprint", "reported_mask_hash", "central_hash"):
   184	        top = manifest.get(key)
   185	        if not top:
   186	            raise FpsGateError(f"manifest: header '{key}' missing")
   187	        for e in manifest["endpoints"]:
   188	            if e.get(key) not in (None, top):
   189	                raise FpsGateError(
   190	                    f"manifest: endpoint {e.get('band')}_{e.get('endpoint')} '{key}'"
   191	                    f" {e.get(key)} != header {top}")
   192	    if manifest["layout_fingerprint"] != layout_fingerprint():
   193	        raise FpsGateError("manifest: layout_fingerprint != canonical FPS 285-bin layout")
   194	    return True
   195	
   196	
   197	def require_footing(manifest, required_bkg_mode=None):
   198	    """Every entry carries the fixed estimator footing; if required_bkg_mode is given, EVERY
   199	    entry's bkg_mode must equal it (this is how the publication path rejects purity controls)."""
   200	    for e in manifest["endpoints"]:
   201	        foot = e.get("footing", {})
   202	        for k, v in REQUIRED_FOOTING.items():
   203	            if foot.get(k) != v:
   204	                raise FpsGateError(
   205	                    f"footing[{e.get('band')}_{e.get('endpoint')}].{k}={foot.get(k)} != {v}")
   206	        if "bkg_mode" not in foot:
   207	            raise FpsGateError(
   208	                f"footing[{e.get('band')}_{e.get('endpoint')}] has no bkg_mode (unprovable)")
   209	        if required_bkg_mode is not None and foot["bkg_mode"] != required_bkg_mode:
   210	            raise FpsGateError(
   211	                f"footing[{e.get('band')}_{e.get('endpoint')}].bkg_mode="
   212	                f"{foot['bkg_mode']} != required {required_bkg_mode}")
   213	    return True

   216	def classify_manifest(manifest):
   217	    """Return 'publication' iff all 10 are negweight-refined, 'purity-control' iff all purity.
   218	    A mixed or unknown-mode manifest fails closed."""
   219	    require_manifest_inventory(manifest)
   220	    modes = {e.get("footing", {}).get("bkg_mode") for e in manifest["endpoints"]}

   223	    if modes == {CONTROL_BKG_MODE}:
   224	        return "purity-control"
   225	    raise FpsGateError(f"manifest classify: non-uniform/unknown bkg modes {sorted(map(str, modes))}")

   227	
   228	def _reject_deferred(manifest):
   229	    """No field anywhere in a publication manifest may be deferred / partial / placeholder."""
   230	    def walk(x, path):
   231	        if isinstance(x, str):
   232	            for mark in DEFERRED_MARKERS:
   233	                if mark in x:

   238	        elif isinstance(x, list):
   239	            for i, v in enumerate(x):
   240	                walk(v, f"{path}[{i}]")

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Blocker 2 (Agent C): fail-closed active five-band selection-complete FPS lateral rollup. Replaces
     3	the ad-hoc `analyze_universes_nd.py --glob` invocation with a hardened, manifest-gated builder.
     4	
     5	REQUIRES a PUBLICATION manifest (fps_provenance.require_publication_manifest -> exactly 10 entries,
     6	exact band/index inventory, common layout/mask/central hashes, and bkg_mode=negweight-refined for
     7	ALL ten). It then, on the 266-bin reported mask (CV>0, hash bound to the manifest):
     8	  - reads each endpoint's hXSecND_flat, subtracts the central CV,
     9	  - forms each band's MAT biased-1/N mean-centered covariance C_b = (1/N) Z Z^T  (N=2 endpoints),
    10	  - requires EXACTLY the five named bands, each with a finite nonzero trace,
    11	  - requires active_total == sum(5 bands) within tol,
    12	and writes hCov_universe4d_total + per-band hCov_universe4d_<band> + provenance to
    13	active_scalar_lateral_fps_cov.root. A zero or incomplete active result fails closed.
    14	
    15	NOT RUN in the 2026-07-18 repair round (no negweight-refined inputs, no holder); this is the gated
    16	producer for the later, verifier-approved C turn.
    17	
    18	  python build_active_lateral_fps.py --manifest <publication_manifest.json> \
    19	      --cv uq_fps/universe_sweep/..._CV.root \
    20	      --out active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/active_scalar_lateral_fps_cov.root
    21	"""
    22	import argparse
    23	import json
    24	import os
    25	
    26	import numpy as np
    27	import ROOT
    28	
    29	import fps_provenance as fp
    30	
    31	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    32	
    33	
    34	def load_flat(path):
    35	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    36	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    37	        raise fp.FpsGateError(f"cannot open {path}")
    38	    h = f.Get("hXSecND_flat")
    39	    if not h:
    40	        f.Close(); raise fp.FpsGateError(f"no hXSecND_flat in {path}")
    41	    a = np.array([h.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(h.GetNbinsX())])
    42	    f.Close()
    43	    if a.size != fp.NBINS_EXT:
    44	        raise fp.FpsGateError(f"{path}: {a.size} bins != {fp.NBINS_EXT}")
    45	    return a
    46	
    47	
    48	def main():
    49	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    50	    ap.add_argument("--manifest", required=True, help="PUBLICATION (negweight-refined) endpoint manifest")
    51	    ap.add_argument("--pass-receipt", required=True, help="hash-bound PASS receipt for --manifest")
    52	    ap.add_argument("--cv", required=True, help="central CV unfold (defines the 285->266 reported mask)")
    53	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/active_scalar_lateral_fps_cov.root")
    54	    ap.add_argument("--tol", type=float, default=1e-10)
    55	    a = ap.parse_args()
    56	
    57	    manifest = json.load(open(a.manifest))
    58	    fp.require_publication_manifest(manifest)          # schema/label/inventory/fingerprints/negweight/mask
    59	    fp.require_pass_receipt(json.load(open(a.pass_receipt)), fp.sha256_file(a.manifest))
    60	    fp.require_no_path_alias(a.out, a.cv, *[e["unfold_root"] for e in manifest["endpoints"]])
    61	
    62	    # central + reported mask (CV>0), bound to the manifest's declared mask hash
    63	    if fp.sha256_file(a.cv) != manifest["central_cv_sha256"]:
    64	        raise fp.FpsGateError("CV sha256 != manifest central_cv_sha256")
    65	    cv = load_flat(a.cv)
    66	    rep = cv > 0
    67	    mh = fp.require_reported_mask(rep)      # RECOMPUTE from CV>0; require == canonical 266/285
    68	    if manifest["reported_mask_hash"] != mh:
    69	        raise fp.FpsGateError(
    70	            f"manifest reported_mask_hash {manifest['reported_mask_hash']} != recomputed canonical {mh} "
    71	            "(central changed; covariance would be built against the wrong reporting mask)")
    72	    cv_rep = cv[rep]
    73	    n_rep = int(rep.sum())
    74	
    75	    by = {e["band"]: {} for e in manifest["endpoints"]}
    76	    for e in manifest["endpoints"]:
    77	        by[e["band"]][e["endpoint"]] = load_flat(e["unfold_root"])[rep] - cv_rep
    78	
    79	    per_band, total = {}, np.zeros((n_rep, n_rep))
    80	    for b in fp.BANDS:
    81	        if set(by[b]) != {0, 1}:
    82	            raise fp.FpsGateError(f"band {b}: endpoints != {{0,1}} (got {sorted(by[b])})")
    83	        D = np.stack([by[b][0], by[b][1]], axis=0)
    84	        Z = D - D.mean(axis=0, keepdims=True)
    85	        cov = (Z.T @ Z) / D.shape[0]              # MAT biased 1/N, mean-centered

    91	
    92	    fo = ROOT.TFile.Open(a.out, "RECREATE")
    93	
    94	    def wcov(name, mat, title=None):
    95	        h = ROOT.TH2D(name, title or name, n_rep, 0, n_rep, n_rep, 0, n_rep)
    96	        for i in range(n_rep):
    97	            for j in range(n_rep):
    98	                h.SetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1, float(mat[i, j]))
    99	        h.Write()

   101	    wcov("hCov_universe4d_total", total, "active selection-complete FPS lateral (sum of 5 bands)")

   103	        wcov(f"hCov_universe4d_{b}", per_band[b])

   107	        "reported_mask_hash": mh, "layout_fingerprint": fp.layout_fingerprint(),

   114	    for b in fp.BANDS:
   115	        print(f"   {b:22s} sqrt_tr={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(per_band[b]),0)):.4e}")
   116

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""P4-FPS validation (Agent C): the selection-complete active scalar FPS lateral covariance
     3	vs the support-limited (dump-all-bank) FPS lateral block. Self-contained FPS sibling of

     9	  p4_validate_active_lateral_fps.py \
    10	    --active active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/active_scalar_lateral_fps_cov.root:hCov_universe4d_total \
    11	    --support uq_fps/corrected/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined.root \

    13	    --out active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/p4_active_lateral_fps_summary.json
    14	"""
    15	import argparse, json, os, sys
    16	import numpy as np
    17	import ROOT
    18	
    19	import fps_provenance as fp
    20	
    21	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    22	# the 5 selection-complete lateral bands (muon angle / resolution / energy scales); the
    23	# support-limited comparator is the sum of these per-band blocks in the FPS combined ROOT.
    24	BANDS = ["BeamAngleX", "BeamAngleY", "MuonResolution",
    25	         "Muon_Energy_MINERvA", "Muon_Energy_MINOS"]
    26	
    27	
    28	def load_th2(spec):
    29	    path, key = spec.rsplit(":", 1)
    30	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    31	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    32	        raise SystemExit(f"cannot open {path}")
    33	    h = f.Get(key)
    34	    if not h:
    35	        raise SystemExit(f"missing key {key} in {path}")
    36	    n = h.GetNbinsX()
    37	    C = np.empty((n, n))
    38	    for i in range(n):
    39	        for j in range(n):
    40	            C[i, j] = h.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1)
    41	    f.Close()
    42	    return C
    43	
    44	
    45	def load_th2_key(path, key):
    46	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    47	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    48	        return None
    49	    h = f.Get(key)
    50	    if not h:
    51	        f.Close(); return None
    52	    n = h.GetNbinsX()
    53	    C = np.empty((n, n))
    54	    for i in range(n):
    55	        for j in range(n):
    56	            C[i, j] = h.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1)
    57	    f.Close()
    58	    return C
    59	
    60	
    61	def mat_gates(C, tag):
    62	    r = {}
    63	    r["shape"] = list(C.shape)
    64	    r["all_finite"] = bool(np.all(np.isfinite(C)))
    65	    asym = np.max(np.abs(C - C.T)) / max(1e-300, np.max(np.abs(C)))
    66	    r["rel_asymmetry"] = float(asym)
    67	    Cs = 0.5 * (C + C.T)
    68	    ev = np.linalg.eigvalsh(Cs)
    69	    r["min_eig"] = float(ev[0]); r["max_eig"] = float(ev[-1])
    70	    r["min_over_max_eig"] = float(ev[0] / max(1e-300, ev[-1]))
    71	    r["psd"] = bool(ev[0] >= -1e-12 * abs(ev[-1]))
    72	    d = np.diag(C)
    73	    r["diag_finite_nonneg"] = bool(np.all(np.isfinite(d)) and np.all(d >= -1e-30))
    74	    r["sqrt_trace"] = float(np.sqrt(np.clip(np.trace(Cs), 0, None)))
    75	    r["n_reported"] = int(np.sum(d > 0))
    76	    print(f"[{tag}] shape={r['shape']} finite={r['all_finite']} "
    77	          f"asym={asym:.2e} min/max_eig={r['min_over_max_eig']:.2e} PSD={r['psd']} "
    78	          f"sqrt_tr={r['sqrt_trace']:.4e}")
    79	    return r
    80	
    81	
    82	def main():
    83	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    84	    ap.add_argument("--active", required=True, help="ROOT:key of the active FPS lateral total cov")
    85	    ap.add_argument("--support", required=True, help="uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined.root")
    86	    ap.add_argument("--band-prefix", default="hCov_universe4d_",
    87	                    help="per-band block key prefix in BOTH the support and active ROOTs")
    88	    ap.add_argument("--manifest", default=None,
    89	                    help="PUBLICATION endpoint manifest; if given, require negweight-refined + mask binding")
    90	    ap.add_argument("--pass-receipt", default=None,
    91	                    help="hash-bound PASS receipt for --manifest (required when --manifest is given)")
    92	    ap.add_argument("--audit-json", default="active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/audit_merged_fps.json",
    93	                    help="merged-endpoint audit receipt to fingerprint into the summary")
    94	    ap.add_argument("--require-publication", action="store_true",
    95	                    help="fail unless a valid publication manifest + PASS receipt are supplied")
    96	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True)
    97	    a = ap.parse_args()
    98	
    99	    out = {"gates": {}, "active": {}, "support": {}, "comparison": {}, "provenance": {}}
   100	    fails = []
   101	
   102	    # ---- fail-closed publication manifest + PASS-receipt gate (blockers 2/3): negweight-refined,
   103	    # canonical mask binding, hash-bound receipt certifying THIS manifest
   104	    manifest = None
   105	    if a.require_publication and not a.manifest:
   106	        fails.append("--require-publication set but no --manifest supplied")
   107	    if a.manifest:
   108	        try:
   109	            manifest = json.load(open(a.manifest))
   110	            fp.require_publication_manifest(manifest)
   111	            if not a.pass_receipt:
   112	                raise fp.FpsGateError("--manifest given without --pass-receipt")
   113	            fp.require_pass_receipt(json.load(open(a.pass_receipt)), fp.sha256_file(a.manifest))
   114	            out["provenance"]["manifest_class"] = fp.classify_manifest(manifest)
   115	            out["provenance"]["reported_mask_hash"] = manifest["reported_mask_hash"]
   116	            out["provenance"]["manifest_sha256"] = fp.sha256_file(a.manifest)
   117	        except fp.FpsGateError as e:
   118	            fails.append(f"manifest/receipt gate: {e}")
   119	    else:
   120	        out["provenance"]["manifest"] = "ABSENT (publication run must pass --manifest + --pass-receipt)"
   121	
   122	    # ---- merged-endpoint audit fingerprint (blocker 3)
   123	    if os.path.exists(a.audit_json):
   124	        out["provenance"]["audit_json_sha256"] = fp.sha256_file(a.audit_json)
   125	        try:
   126	            audit = json.load(open(a.audit_json))
   127	            if audit.get("result") != "PASS":
   128	                fails.append(f"merged-endpoint audit not PASS (result={audit.get('result')})")
   129	        except Exception as e:
   130	            fails.append(f"audit json unreadable: {e}")
   131	    else:
   132	        fails.append(f"merged-endpoint audit receipt absent: {a.audit_json}")
   133	
   134	    Cact = load_th2(a.active)
   135	    out["active"] = mat_gates(Cact, "active(FPS selection-complete lateral)")
   136	
   137	    # ---- exact 5-band active inventory + nonzero per-band traces + total identity (blocker 3)
   138	    a_root = a.active.rsplit(":", 1)[0]
   139	    active_bands = {}
   140	    for b in fp.BANDS:
   141	        cb = load_th2_key(a_root, f"{a.band_prefix}{b}")
   142	        if cb is None:
   143	            fails.append(f"active per-band block '{a.band_prefix}{b}' absent")
   144	        else:
   145	            active_bands[b] = cb

   147	        try:
   148	            fp.check_active_rollup(active_bands, Cact)   # 5 nonzero bands + total==sum(5)
   149	            out["active"]["rollup_identity"] = "PASS (total == sum of 5 nonzero bands)"
   150	        except fp.FpsGateError as e:
   151	            fails.append(f"active rollup: {e}")
   152	
   153	    # hard gates on the active P4-FPS product
   154	    for g in ("all_finite", "psd", "diag_finite_nonneg"):
   155	        if not out["active"][g]:
   156	            fails.append(f"active {g}")
   157	    if out["active"]["rel_asymmetry"] > 1e-9:
   158	        fails.append("active asymmetry >1e-9")
   159	    if out["active"]["sqrt_trace"] <= 0 or out["active"]["n_reported"] == 0:
   160	        fails.append("active is zero/empty (nothing migrated into the covariance)")
   161	
   162	    # support-limited block = sum of the 5 kinematic per-band covs in the FPS combined ROOT
   163	    Csup = None; present = []
   164	    for b in BANDS:
   165	        cb = load_th2_key(a.support, f"{a.band_prefix}{b}")
   166	        if cb is None:
   167	            print(f"[support] band {b} key '{a.band_prefix}{b}' absent"); continue
   168	        present.append(b)
   169	        Csup = cb if Csup is None else Csup + cb
   170	    out["support"]["bands_present"] = present
   171	    if set(present) != set(BANDS):
   172	        fails.append(f"support inventory != 5 named bands (present={present}); check --band-prefix")
   173	    if Csup is None:
   174	        fails.append("no support-limited lateral bands found (check --band-prefix)")
   175	    else:
   176	        out["support"].update(mat_gates(Csup, "support(sum of 5 FPS bands)"))
   177	        if Csup.shape == Cact.shape:
   178	            st_a = out["active"]["sqrt_trace"]; st_s = out["support"]["sqrt_trace"]
   179	            da = np.sqrt(np.clip(np.diag(Cact), 0, None))
   180	            ds = np.sqrt(np.clip(np.diag(Csup), 0, None))
   181	            m = ds > 0
   182	            ratio = np.divide(da, ds, out=np.zeros_like(da), where=m)
   183	            out["comparison"] = {
   184	                "sqrt_trace_active": st_a,
   185	                "sqrt_trace_support": st_s,
   186	                "sqrt_trace_ratio_active_over_support": float(st_a / max(1e-300, st_s)),
   187	                "per_bin_sigma_ratio_median": float(np.median(ratio[m])),
   188	                "per_bin_sigma_ratio_p16": float(np.percentile(ratio[m], 16)),
   189	                "per_bin_sigma_ratio_p84": float(np.percentile(ratio[m], 84)),
   190	                "n_bins_active_gt_support": int(np.sum(da > ds)),
   191	                "n_bins_common": int(np.sum(m)),
   192	            }
   193	            print(f"[compare] sqrt_tr active={st_a:.4e} support={st_s:.4e} "
   194	                  f"ratio={st_a/max(1e-300,st_s):.3f}; per-bin median ratio "
   195	                  f"{out['comparison']['per_bin_sigma_ratio_median']:.3f}")
   196	        else:
   197	            fails.append(f"active/support shape mismatch {Cact.shape} vs {Csup.shape}")
   198	
   199	    out["gates"]["fails"] = fails
   200	    out["gates"]["result"] = "PASS" if not fails else "FAIL"
   201	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(a.out), exist_ok=True)
   202	    with open(a.out, "w") as fh:
   203	        json.dump(out, fh, indent=2)
   204	    print(f"\nRESULT {out['gates']['result']}" + ("" if not fails else " :: " + "; ".join(fails)))
   205	    print(f"summary -> {a.out}")
   206	    sys.exit(0 if not fails else 1)
   207	
   208	
   209	if __name__ == "__main__":
   210	    main()

     2	"""P6-FPS FINAL lateral adoption (Agent C): swap the support-limited FPS lateral block for the
     3	selection-complete active scalar lateral block in the combined FPS covariance, by PURE COMPONENT
     4	SUM (never subtraction -- the D5 lesson: subtracting a DIFFERENT-estimator block breaks PSD; here
     5	we simply rebuild the combined by summing PSD pieces).
     6	
     7	    C_comb_active = SUM_{band in universe4d blocks EXCEPT total and the 5 lateral} C_band   (incl.
     8	                    __Normalization_flat, MinosEfficiency, and every vertical/model/hadronic band)
     9	                  + C_stat (external uq_cov_stat_fps.root:hCov_statfps_reported)
    10	                  + C_ML   (external uq_cov_mlsplit_fps.root:hCov_mlsplitfps_reported)
    11	                  + C_active_lateral (active_scalar_lateral_fps_cov.root:hCov_universe4d_total)
    12	
    13	Writes an intermediate combined ROOT that (a) carries the rebuilt hCov_combined4d_total, (b) copies
    14	EVERY original per-band hCov_universe4d_<band> block through unchanged (so the downstream
    15	adopt_unified_4d.py can still read the 13 vertical per-band carriers), and (c) records the active
    16	lateral total under hCov_universe4d_active_lateral_total. A subtraction-based cross-check
    17	(C_comb_orig - SUM(5 support lateral) + C_active) is reported for consistency but is NOT the product.
    18	
    19	Then run the HARDENED unified-throw adopt (adopt_unified_fps.py) on THIS file for the publishable cov:
    20	  python adopt_unified_fps.py --uthrow uq_fps/corrected/unified_throw_cov_fps.root \
    21	      --combined <this --out> --prod uq_fps/universe_sweep/fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_CV.root \
    22	      --out uq_fps/corrected/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined_uthrow_activelat.root
    23	
    24	  python adopt_active_lateral_fps.py    # defaults below
    25	"""
    26	import argparse
    27	import json
    28	import os
    29	
    30	import numpy as np
    31	import ROOT
    32	
    33	import fps_provenance as fp
    34	
    35	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    36	LATERAL = ["BeamAngleX", "BeamAngleY", "MuonResolution",
    37	           "Muon_Energy_MINERvA", "Muon_Energy_MINOS"]
    38	PURE_SUM_SUB_TOL = 1e-9   # pure-sum must equal same-source subtraction to float precision
    39	
    40	
    41	def _th2(h):
    42	    n = h.GetNbinsX()
    43	    C = np.empty((n, n))
    44	    for i in range(n):
    45	        for j in range(n):
    46	            C[i, j] = h.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1)
    47	    return C
    48	
    49	
    50	def _load_key(path, key, required=True):
    51	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    52	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    53	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] cannot open {path}")
    54	    h = f.Get(key)
    55	    if not h:
    56	        f.Close()
    57	        if required:
    58	            raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] missing {key} in {path}")
    59	        return None
    60	    C = _th2(h)
    61	    f.Close()
    62	    return C
    63	
    64	
    65	def main():
    66	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    67	    ap.add_argument("--combined",
    68	                    default="uq_fps/corrected/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined.root")
    69	    ap.add_argument("--active",
    70	                    default="active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/active_scalar_lateral_fps_cov.root:hCov_universe4d_total")
    71	    ap.add_argument("--stat", default="uq_fps/corrected/uq_cov_stat_fps.root:hCov_statfps_reported")
    72	    ap.add_argument("--ml", default="uq_fps/corrected/uq_cov_mlsplit_fps.root:hCov_mlsplitfps_reported")
    73	    ap.add_argument("--manifest", required=True, help="hash-bound PUBLICATION manifest")
    74	    ap.add_argument("--pass-receipt", required=True, help="hash-bound PASS receipt for --manifest")
    75	    ap.add_argument("--out",

    79	    # blocker 2: hash-bound publication manifest + PASS receipt mandatory at this transition

    83

    85	    fp.require_no_path_alias(a.out, a.combined, a.active.rsplit(":", 1)[0],

    94	    fc = ROOT.TFile.Open(a.combined)

    98	    C_nonlat = np.zeros((n, n))
    99	    lateral_seen, C_support_lat = [], np.zeros((n, n))
   100	    for k in fc.GetListOfKeys():
   101	        name = k.GetName()
   102	        if not name.startswith("hCov_universe4d_"):
   103	            continue
   104	        band = name[len("hCov_universe4d_"):]
   105	        if band == "total":

   107	        mat = _th2(fc.Get(name))

   122	    st_path, st_key = a.stat.rsplit(":", 1)
   123	    ml_path, ml_key = a.ml.rsplit(":", 1)
   124	    C_stat = _load_key(st_path, st_key)
   125	    C_ml = _load_key(ml_path, ml_key)

   128	            raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] {tag} shape {C.shape} != {(n, n)}")
   129	
   130	    # PURE-SUM product
   131	    C_comb_active = C_nonlat + C_stat + C_ml + C_active
   132	    C_comb_active = 0.5 * (C_comb_active + C_comb_active.T)
   133	
   134	    # subtraction cross-check (NOT the product): should match to float precision iff the stored
   135	    # combined == SUM(all bands)+norm+stat+ML with the same external stat/ML the budget used.
   136	    C_check = C_comb_orig - C_support_lat + C_active
   137	    max_abs_diff = float(np.max(np.abs(C_comb_active - C_check)))
   138	    rel_diff = max_abs_diff / max(1e-300, float(np.max(np.abs(C_comb_active))))
   139	    # blocker 4: HARD gate -- the PSD-safe pure sum must equal the same-source subtraction to float
   140	    # precision, else the stored combined is not a clean SUM(bands)+stat+ML and the swap is unsafe.
   141	    fp.require_pure_sum_matches_sub(C_comb_active, C_check, tol=PURE_SUM_SUB_TOL)

   146	    sa = np.sqrt(np.clip(np.diag(C_active), 0, None))
   147	    ss = np.sqrt(np.clip(np.diag(C_support_lat), 0, None))
   148	    print(f"[swap] reported bins            = {n}")
   149	    print(f"[swap] non-lateral bands summed = {len(band_blocks) - len(lateral_seen)}  "
   150	          f"(+ stat + ML + active lateral)")
   151	    print(f"[swap] lateral sqrt-tr: support = {np.sqrt(np.trace(C_support_lat)):.4e}  "

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Blocker 5 (Agent C): hardened FPS unified-throw final adoption. FPS wrapper around the
     3	adopt_unified_4d.py math (C_final = C_pre - C_vertical + (g g^T) o C_vertical) with fail-closed
     4	gates so the publishable covariance is reproducible and cannot be built from mismatched inputs.
     5	
     6	Gates (fps_provenance):
     7	  - reported dim/order equality across combined (C_pre), unified-throw, and CV-defined mask;
     8	  - reject diag(C_blocksum)<=0 < diag(C_unified) (ill-defined inflation / mask-estimator mismatch);
     9	  - reject output aliasing any input;
    10	  - require the written C_final to satisfy the reconstruction identity to float precision;
    11	  - PSD at the declared tolerance.
    12	Preserves hJointMeanShift SEPARATELY, labels the mean-centered policy, and writes C_vertical + g +
    13	source hashes so C_final is reproducible from the final ROOT plus this inseparable provenance.
    14	
    15	NOT RUN in the 2026-07-18 repair round (no active-lateral combined exists yet; gated on the
    16	verifier-approved negweight-refined chain).
    17	
    18	  python adopt_unified_fps.py \
    19	      --combined uq_fps/corrected/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined_activelat.root \
    20	      --uthrow  uq_fps/corrected/unified_throw_cov_fps.root \
    21	      --prod    uq_fps/universe_sweep/fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_CV.root \
    22	      --out     uq_fps/corrected/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined_uthrow_activelat.root
    23	"""
    24	import argparse
    25	import json
    26	
    27	import numpy as np
    28	import ROOT
    29	
    30	import fps_provenance as fp
    31	
    32	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    33	# the 13 vertical bands the unified throw covers (12 knobs + Flux); the lateral bands are orthogonal
    34	# (added components) and pass through the adoption untouched.
    35	VERT_BANDS = ["2p2h", "CCQEPauliSupViaKF", "FrAbs_pi", "FrElas_N", "HighQ2", "LowQ2",
    36	              "MaCCQE", "MaRES", "MFP_N", "MvRES", "Rvn2pi", "Rvp2pi", "Flux"]
    37	PSD_TOL = -1e-12
    38	
    39	
    40	def _th2(h):
    41	    n = h.GetNbinsX()
    42	    C = np.empty((n, n))
    43	    for i in range(n):
    44	        for j in range(n):
    45	            C[i, j] = h.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1)
    46	    return C
    47	
    48	
    49	def _get(f, key, required=True):
    50	    h = f.Get(key)
    51	    if not h and required:
    52	        raise fp.FpsGateError(f"missing {key}")
    53	    return h
    54	
    55	
    56	def main():
    57	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    58	    ap.add_argument("--combined", required=True, help="active-lateral pre-uthrow combined ROOT")
    59	    ap.add_argument("--uthrow", required=True, help="unified_throw_cov_fps.root")
    60	    ap.add_argument("--prod", required=True, help="CV unfold (defines reported mask/central)")
    61	    ap.add_argument("--manifest", required=True, help="hash-bound PUBLICATION manifest")
    62	    ap.add_argument("--pass-receipt", required=True, help="hash-bound PASS receipt for --manifest")
    63	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True)
    64	    a = ap.parse_args()
    65	
    66	    # blocker 2: hash-bound publication manifest + PASS receipt mandatory at this transition
    67	    manifest = json.load(open(a.manifest))
    68	    fp.require_publication_manifest(manifest)
    69	    fp.require_pass_receipt(json.load(open(a.pass_receipt)), fp.sha256_file(a.manifest))
    70	    fp.require_no_path_alias(a.out, a.combined, a.uthrow, a.prod)
    71	
    72	    fu = ROOT.TFile.Open(a.uthrow)
    73	    C_uni = _th2(_get(fu, "C_unified"))
    74	    C_block = _th2(_get(fu, "C_blocksum"))
    75	    # blocker 5: hJointMeanShift is MANDATORY (not optional); validate present/finite/dim
    76	    hms = _get(fu, "hJointMeanShift", required=True)
    77	    mean_shift = np.array([hms.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(hms.GetNbinsX())])
    78	    fu.Close()
    79	    fp.require_mean_shift(mean_shift)
    80	
    81	    fc = ROOT.TFile.Open(a.combined)
    82	    C_comb = _th2(_get(fc, "hCov_combined4d_total"))          # C_pre (active-lateral combined)
    83	    C_vert = None
    84	    for b in VERT_BANDS:
    85	        C_vert = _th2(_get(fc, f"hCov_universe4d_{b}")) if C_vert is None \
    86	            else C_vert + _th2(_get(fc, f"hCov_universe4d_{b}"))
    87	    fc.Close()
    88	
    89	    fpr = ROOT.TFile.Open(a.prod)
    90	    hx = _get(fpr, "hXSecND_flat")
    91	    xfull = np.array([hx.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(hx.GetNbinsX())])
    92	    fpr.Close()
    93	    rep = xfull > 0
    94	    n = int(rep.sum())
    95	
    96	    # ---- dim/order equality across all inputs (blocker 5)
    97	    for tag, C in (("C_unified", C_uni), ("C_blocksum", C_block),
    98	                   ("combined", C_comb), ("C_vertical", C_vert)):
    99	        fp.require_square_finite(C, expected_dim=n, tag=tag)
   100	
   101	    vu = np.clip(np.diag(C_uni), 0, None)
   102	    vb = np.clip(np.diag(C_block), 0, None)
   103	    # ---- reject zero-block / nonzero-unified (blocker 5)
   104	    fp.require_unified_inputs(vb, vu, tag="unified-vs-block")
   105	
   106	    s_adopt = np.sqrt(np.maximum(vu, vb))
   107	    sb = np.sqrt(vb)
   108	    g = np.ones(n)
   109	    m = sb > 0
   110	    g[m] = s_adopt[m] / sb[m]                                  # >= 1
   111	    G = np.outer(g, g)
   112	    C_new = (C_comb - C_vert) + G * C_vert
   113	    C_new = 0.5 * (C_new + C_new.T)
   114	
   115	    # ---- identity + PSD gates (blocker 5)
   116	    fp.require_final_identity(C_new, C_comb, C_vert, g, tag="FPS uthrow adoption")
   117	    fp.require_psd(C_new, tol=PSD_TOL, tag="C_final")
   118	
   119	    fo = ROOT.TFile.Open(a.out, "RECREATE")
   120	
   121	    def wcov(name, mat, title=None):
   122	        h = ROOT.TH2D(name, title or name, n, 0, n, n, 0, n)
   123	        for i in range(n):
   124	            for j in range(n):
   125	                h.SetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1, float(mat[i, j]))
   126	        h.Write()
   127	
   128	    wcov("hCov_combined4d_total_uthrow", C_new,
   129	         "PUBLISHABLE FPS cov (active lateral + stat + ML + unified-throw vertical inflation)")
   130	    wcov("hCov_vertical_sweep", C_vert, "C_vertical (sum of 13 vertical per-band covs; adoption carrier)")
   131	    hg = ROOT.TH1D("hInflation_g", "per-bin unified/block sigma inflation g>=1", n, 0, n)
   132	    for i in range(n):
   133	        hg.SetBinContent(i + 1, float(g[i]))
   134	    hg.Write()
   135	    # preserve hJointMeanShift SEPARATELY (mandatory; mean-centered policy -> NOT folded into C_final)
   136	    hjm = ROOT.TH1D("hJointMeanShift", "joint mean shift (preserved; NOT added: mean-centered policy)",
   137	                    mean_shift.size, 0, mean_shift.size)
   138	    for i in range(mean_shift.size):
   139	        hjm.SetBinContent(i + 1, float(mean_shift[i]))
   140	    hjm.Write()
   141	    prov = {
   142	        "identity": "C_final = C_pre - C_vertical + (g g^T) o C_vertical",
   143	        "C_pre": "hCov_combined4d_total from --combined (active-lateral)",
   144	        "C_vertical": "hCov_vertical_sweep (written here) = sum of 13 vertical per-band covs",
   145	        "g": "hInflation_g (written here)",
   146	        "centering": "mean-centered (diag C_unified only); hJointMeanShift preserved separately, NOT added",
   147	        "reported_dim": n, "layout_fingerprint": fp.layout_fingerprint(),
   148	        "source_sha256": {"combined": fp.sha256_file(a.combined),
   149	                          "uthrow": fp.sha256_file(a.uthrow),
   150	                          "prod": fp.sha256_file(a.prod)},
   151	        "reproducible_from_final_root": True,
   152	    }
   153	    ROOT.TNamed("provenance", json.dumps(prov)).Write()
   154	    fo.Close()
   155	    fpr_ = fp.cov_fingerprint(C_new)
   156	    print(f"[uthrow-fps] wrote {a.out}")
   157	    print(f"[uthrow-fps] dim={fpr_['dim']} rank={fpr_['rank']} sqrt_tr={fpr_['sqrt_trace']:.4e} "
   158	          f"min/max_eig={fpr_['min_over_max_eig']:.2e}  g: median={np.median(g):.3f} max={g.max():.2f}")
   159	
   160	
   161	if __name__ == "__main__":
   162	    main()

    24	[[ "${BKG_MODE}" == "negweight-refined" ]] || { echo "[FAIL] publication driver requires BKG_MODE=negweight-refined (got '${BKG_MODE}')"; exit 2; }
    25	MERGEDIR="${ND}/active_universe_5d/fps/merged"
    26	OUTDIR="${ND}/active_universe_5d/fps/unfolds_${BKG_MODE//-/_}"; mkdir -p "${OUTDIR}"
    27	echo "[p4fps-unfold] JOBID=${JOBID} CONC=${CONC} CPT=${CPT} bkg=${BKG_MODE} ns=${OUTDIR} start $(date -u '+%F %T UTC')"
    28	for BAND in "${BANDS[@]}"; do
    29	  for EP in 0 1; do
    30	    MERGED="${MERGEDIR}/runEventLoopOmniFold_5D_FPS_active_${BAND}_${EP}_universes_full.root"
    31	    OUT="${OUTDIR}/fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_${BAND}_${EP}.root"
    32	    # skip-if-COMPLETE: validate the existing output on the compute node (login node has no ROOT).
    33	    # A wall-truncated .root passes `-s` but fails completeness -> remove & redo (never skip a bad file).
    34	    if [[ -s "${OUT}" ]]; then
    35	      if srun --jobid="${JOBID}" --overlap --exact -n1 -c2 bash -lc \
    36	           "export HOME=/global/homes/j/josephrb ROOT628_PREFIX=/global/homes/j/josephrb/.conda/envs/root_6_28; source '${REPO}/setup_salloc_env.sh' >/dev/null 2>&1; cd '${ND}' && python3 fps_unfold_complete.py '${OUT}'" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    37	        echo "[SKIP] ${BAND}:${EP} (validated complete)"; continue
    38	      fi
    39	      echo "[REDO] ${BAND}:${EP} (present but incomplete/truncated -> removing)"; rm -f "${OUT}"
    40	    fi
    41	    if [[ ! -s "${MERGED}" ]]; then echo "[WAIT] ${BAND}:${EP} merged ROOT absent"; continue; fi
    42	    while [ "$(jobs -rp | wc -l)" -ge "${CONC}" ]; do sleep 8; done
    43	    srun --jobid="${JOBID}" --overlap --exact -n1 -c"${CPT}" bash -lc "
    44	      export HOME=/global/homes/j/josephrb OMP_NUM_THREADS=${CPT} \
    45	        ROOT628_PREFIX=/global/homes/j/josephrb/.conda/envs/root_6_28
    46	      source '${REPO}/setup_salloc_env.sh' >/dev/null 2>&1
    47	      cd '${ND}'
    48	      TMP='${OUT}.tmp'
    49	      python3 unfold_nd_omnifold_unbinned.py --omnifile '${MERGED}' --mcfile '${FLUX_MC}' --axes '' \

    51	        --iters 5 --use-weights --estimator lgbm --seed 42 --bkg-mode '${BKG_MODE}' --out \"\$TMP\" --verbose \
    52	        > '${OUTDIR}/unfold_${BAND}_${EP}.log' 2>&1 \
    53	        && mv -f \"\$TMP\" '${OUT}' \
    54	        && echo '[done] ${BAND}:${EP} '\$(date -u +%T) \
    55	        || echo '[FAIL] ${BAND}:${EP} rc='\$? ' (see ${OUTDIR}/unfold_${BAND}_${EP}.log)'" &
    56	    sleep 3
    57	  done
    58	done
    59	wait
    60	# mode-stamped completion/config manifest sidecars (login node; clean JSON, no ROOT). Written only
    61	# for outputs that landed, so a truncated/absent endpoint has no config and cannot enter the rollup.
    62	for BAND in "${BANDS[@]}"; do for EP in 0 1; do
    63	  OUT="${OUTDIR}/fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_${BAND}_${EP}.root"
    64	  [[ -s "${OUT}" ]] || continue
    65	  cat > "${OUT}.config.json" <<EOF
    66	{"band":"${BAND}","endpoint":${EP},"bkg_mode":"${BKG_MODE}","estimator":"lgbm","seed":42,
    67	 "iters":5,"use_weights":true,"full_phase_space":true,"launcher":"run_active_fps_unfolds_interactive.sh"}
    68	EOF
    69	done; done
    70	N=$(find "${OUTDIR}" -name 'fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_*_?.root' -size +0c 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
    71	echo "[p4fps-unfold] all steps returned $(date -u '+%F %T UTC'); unfolds present=${N}/10 (bkg=${BKG_MODE})"
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Completeness gate for FPS active-endpoint unfolds (Agent C). A wall-killed unfold leaves a
     3	present-but-TRUNCATED .root (ROOT auto-recovered on reopen, or the final hXSecND_flat/histograms
     4	never written) -- the exact failure that makes a naive `-s`/existence skip consume a bad file. This
     5	validates each output is COMPLETE so the driver never skips (and the P4 chain never ingests) a
     6	truncated unfold. Exit 0 iff EVERY requested file is complete.
     7	
     8	  fps_unfold_complete.py f1.root [f2 ...]   # explicit files
     9	  fps_unfold_complete.py --all              # the 10 canonical endpoints (relative to CWD=nd-unfolding)
    10	
    11	COMPLETE := TFile opens & not zombie; NOT TFile::kRecovered (recovery => truncated write);
    12	hXSecND_flat present with nbins==285 (FPS 15pt x 19pz extended grid), all bins finite, sum>0;
    13	globalCompleteness TParameter present, finite, > MIN_COMPLETE (the unfold's '[CHECK] c=' value).
    14	"""
    15	import argparse, os, sys
    16	import numpy as np
    17	import ROOT
    18	
    19	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    20	BANDS = ["BeamAngleX", "BeamAngleY", "MuonResolution",
    21	         "Muon_Energy_MINERvA", "Muon_Energy_MINOS"]
    22	OUTDIR = "active_universe_5d/fps/unfolds"
    23	NAME = "fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_{b}_{ep}.root"
    24	EXPECT_NBINS = 285          # 15 (pt) x 19 (pz) FPS extended grid
    25	MIN_COMPLETE = 0.50         # sanity floor; healthy unfolds report c=1.0000
    26	
    27	
    28	def check(path):
    29	    r = {"path": os.path.basename(path), "ok": False, "why": ""}
    30	    if not os.path.exists(path):
    31	        r["why"] = "missing"; return r
    32	    if os.path.getsize(path) < 1024:
    33	        r["why"] = f"tiny ({os.path.getsize(path)}B)"; return r
    34	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    35	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    36	        r["why"] = "zombie/unopenable"; return r
    37	    if f.TestBit(ROOT.TFile.kRecovered):
    38	        f.Close(); r["why"] = "kRecovered (truncated/uncleanly-closed write)"; return r
    39	    h = f.Get("hXSecND_flat")
    40	    if not h:
    41	        f.Close(); r["why"] = "no hXSecND_flat"; return r
    42	    nb = h.GetNbinsX()
    43	    if nb != EXPECT_NBINS:
    44	        f.Close(); r["why"] = f"nbins {nb} != {EXPECT_NBINS}"; return r
    45	    vals = np.array([h.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(nb)])
    46	    if not np.all(np.isfinite(vals)):
    47	        f.Close(); r["why"] = "non-finite bins in hXSecND_flat"; return r
    48	    s = float(vals.sum())
    49	    if s <= 0:
    50	        f.Close(); r["why"] = f"sum<=0 ({s:.3e})"; return r
    51	    gc = f.Get("globalCompleteness")
    52	    gcv = float(gc.GetVal()) if gc else None
    53	    f.Close()
    54	    if gcv is None or not np.isfinite(gcv):
    55	        r["why"] = "no/NaN globalCompleteness"; return r
    56	    if gcv < MIN_COMPLETE:
    57	        r["why"] = f"globalCompleteness {gcv:.4f} < {MIN_COMPLETE}"; return r
    58	    r.update(ok=True, sum=s, gc=gcv, nbins=nb); return r
    59	
    60	
    61	def main():
    62	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    63	    ap.add_argument("files", nargs="*")
    64	    ap.add_argument("--all", action="store_true")
    65	    a = ap.parse_args()
    66	    files = list(a.files)
    67	    if a.all:
    68	        files = [os.path.join(OUTDIR, NAME.format(b=b, ep=ep)) for b in BANDS for ep in (0, 1)]
    69	    if not files:
    70	        sys.exit("no files given")
    71	    nbad = 0
    72	    for p in files:
    73	        r = check(p)
    74	        if r["ok"]:
    75	            print(f"[OK]   {r['path']} nbins={r['nbins']} sum={r['sum']:.4e} gc={r['gc']:.4f}")
    76	        else:
    77	            nbad += 1; print(f"[BAD]  {r['path']} :: {r['why']}")
    78	    print(f"\n{len(files) - nbad}/{len(files)} complete")
    79	    sys.exit(0 if nbad == 0 else 1)
    80	
    81	
    82	if __name__ == "__main__":
    83	    main()

     2	"""Blocker 7 (Agent C): ROOT-free unit tests for the FPS provenance/gate library. Exercises every
     3	fail-closed path the covariance chain depends on: manifest inventory (wrong/missing/extra/dup),
     4	mismatched layout/mask/central/bkg-mode footing, active-rollup band inventory + zero band/total,
     5	pure-sum-vs-subtraction residual, zero-block/nonzero-unified, path aliasing, and the final
     6	adoption identity reconstruction. No ROOT, no compute node: pure numpy + dict fixtures.
     7	
     8	  python -m pytest tests/test_fps_provenance.py     # or:  python tests/test_fps_provenance.py
     9	"""

    20	def _spd(n, seed):
    21	    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    22	    A = rng.standard_normal((n, n))
    23	    return A @ A.T / n + np.eye(n) * 1e-6

    26	def make_manifest(bkg_mode=fp.PUBLICATION_BKG_MODE, drop=None, extra=None, dup=None,
    27	                  bad_layout=False, mixed_mode=False, bad_footing=None):
    28	    lay = "BAD" if bad_layout else fp.layout_fingerprint()
    29	    mh, ch = "maskhash-xyz", "central-xyz"
    30	    eps = []
    31	    pairs = [(b, ep) for b in fp.BANDS for ep in fp.ENDPOINTS]
    32	    if drop:
    33	        pairs = [p for p in pairs if p != drop]
    34	    if extra:
    35	        pairs = pairs + [extra]
    36	    if dup:
    37	        pairs = pairs + [dup]
    38	    for i, (b, ep) in enumerate(pairs):
    39	        foot = dict(fp.REQUIRED_FOOTING)
    40	        foot["bkg_mode"] = bkg_mode
    41	        if mixed_mode and i == 0:
    42	            foot["bkg_mode"] = fp.CONTROL_BKG_MODE
    43	        if bad_footing and (b, ep) == bad_footing[0]:
    44	            foot[bad_footing[1]] = bad_footing[2]
    45	        eps.append({"band": b, "endpoint": ep,
    46	                    "layout_fingerprint": lay, "reported_mask_hash": mh, "central_hash": ch,
    47	                    "footing": foot})
    48	    return {"layout_fingerprint": lay, "reported_mask_hash": mh, "central_hash": ch,
    49	            "endpoints": eps}

   200	def make_pub_manifest(drop_hash=None, deferred_hash=None, label=None, schema=None,
   201	                      bkg_mode=None, mask=None):
   202	    eps = []
   203	    for b in fp.BANDS:
   204	        for ep in fp.ENDPOINTS:
   205	            foot = dict(fp.REQUIRED_FOOTING); foot["bkg_mode"] = bkg_mode or fp.PUBLICATION_BKG_MODE
   206	            e = {"band": b, "endpoint": ep,
   207	                 "layout_fingerprint": fp.layout_fingerprint(),
   208	                 "reported_mask_hash": mask or fp.REPORTED_MASK_FINGERPRINT,
   209	                 "central_hash": "cv", "footing": foot}
   210	            for fld in fp.PUBLICATION_ENDPOINT_HASH_FIELDS:
   211	                e[fld] = H64
   212	            if drop_hash and (b, ep) == (fp.BANDS[0], 0):
   213	                e.pop(drop_hash, None)
   214	            if deferred_hash and (b, ep) == (fp.BANDS[0], 0):
   215	                e[deferred_hash] = "DEFERRED-requires-ROOT"
   216	            eps.append(e)
   217	    return {"schema": schema or fp.PUBLICATION_SCHEMA, "label": label or fp.PUBLICATION_LABEL,
   218	            "layout_fingerprint": fp.layout_fingerprint(),
   219	            "reported_mask_hash": mask or fp.REPORTED_MASK_FINGERPRINT,
   220	            "central_hash": "cv", "endpoints": eps}
